signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.2% | NO | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation rate rose to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% in January and February, below market forecasts of 2.4% and aligning with the Bank of Korea's 2% target amid contained price pressures.[1]  
Slight variations exist across sources (e.g., 2.16% per rateinflation.com), but Trading Economics' March 5,
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Potential near-term catalyst:** The Senate Banking Committee has indicated a window in the latter half of April for mark
signals.raw: Potential near-term catalyst:** The Senate Banking Committee has indicated a window in the latter half of April for markup on the CLARITY Act, which could classify XRP as a digital commodity[3]. However, this is a scheduled legislative event rather than breaking news.
